decision: Tech stack: Next.js 15 + Supabase + Vercel for CraftMarket marketplace
chosen_approach: Next.js 15 App Router with tRPC, Supabase Postgres + Auth, deployed on Vercel

rejected_alternatives.reason: PlanetScale lacks row-level security; Fly.io adds ops overhead for a two-person team.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If Supabase free tier hits connection limits under load

rejected_alternatives.reason: Firebase Firestore pricing unpredictable at scale; team has no Svelte experience.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If React Server Components prove too complex for the catalog page

decision: Scope boundary: shipping carrier API integration deferred to v2
chosen_approach: Manual tracking number entry by sellers; no carrier API integration in v1

rejected_alternatives.approach: Integrate EasyPost for multi-carrier shipping labels
rejected_alternatives.reason: Adds 2-week implementation scope and requires business account setup with each carrier.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If seller onboarding feedback cites manual tracking as top friction point

decision: Monetization model: 8% platform take-rate on each transaction
chosen_approach: Flat 8% commission on order total, deducted from seller payout at release time

rejected_alternatives.reason: Subscription model discourages new sellers from listing; marketplace needs supply-side liquidity first.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If conversion drops below 2% with current take-rate model

rejected_alternatives.reason: Analytics features not in v1 scope; would delay launch by 3+ weeks.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If seller retention drops below 60% after 90 days

revisit_criterion: If conversion drops below 2% with current take-rate model

decision: Data model: orders + items + users on Postgres via Supabase with RLS
chosen_approach: Six core tables (User, Product, Order, OrderItem, Inventory, Payout) on Supabase Postgres with row-level security policies per role

rejected_alternatives.approach: Denormalized document model in Supabase with JSON columns
rejected_alternatives.reason: JSON columns bypass RLS field-level checks and make index-based queries on order status impossible.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If join performance degrades below 50ms p95 on the seller inbox query

decision: Auth: Supabase JWT with httpOnly cookies, no localStorage token storage
chosen_approach: Supabase Auth issues JWTs stored in httpOnly secure cookies; refresh tokens rotate on use with 7-day absolute expiry

rejected_alternatives.approach: Store JWT in localStorage with short expiry
rejected_alternatives.reason: localStorage is accessible to XSS; httpOnly cookies eliminate client-side token exposure entirely.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If SSR hydration issues require client-side token access for initial render

decision: Data model refinement: add idempotency_key column to orders table for retry safety
chosen_approach: Add unique idempotency_key (UUID v4) column to orders table; POST /api/orders requires it; duplicate key returns existing order

rejected_alternatives.approach: Client-side deduplication via localStorage order ID cache
rejected_alternatives.reason: Client cache is unreliable across devices and clears on browser data wipe; server-side uniqueness is the only safe guarantee.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If idempotency key index adds measurable latency to order writes

decision: Auth model: row-level security on all user-scoped tables with deny-by-default
chosen_approach: RLS policies on User, Order, Product, Inventory, Payout tables; default deny; explicit allow rules per role (buyer reads own orders, seller reads assigned orders)

rejected_alternatives.approach: Application-level authorization checks in tRPC middleware only
rejected_alternatives.reason: Middleware-only auth is bypassable via direct database access or Supabase dashboard queries; RLS enforces at the database layer.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If RLS policy complexity causes query planner regressions above 100ms

decision: RLS policy: deny-by-default with explicit allow rules per role
chosen_approach: Each table starts with ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and a default-deny policy; allow rules added per role (buyer, seller, admin) with USING clauses

rejected_alternatives.approach: Allow-by-default with deny rules for sensitive columns
rejected_alternatives.reason: Allow-by-default risks data leaks when new tables are added without deny rules; deny-by-default is safer for a marketplace with PII.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If deny-by-default blocks a legitimate admin dashboard query pattern

decision: Cart timeout: 30 minutes of inactivity before cart items are released back to inventory
chosen_approach: Server-side cart expiry at 30 minutes; background job checks every 5 minutes and releases reserved inventory for expired carts

rejected_alternatives.approach: No cart timeout — items reserved indefinitely until checkout or manual removal
rejected_alternatives.reason: Indefinite reservation lets a single buyer lock up inventory, starving other buyers during high-demand periods.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If cart abandonment rate exceeds 70% due to timeout being too aggressive

rejected_alternatives.approach: Client-side only cart with no server reservation
rejected_alternatives.reason: No reservation means two buyers can check out the same last-in-stock item simultaneously, causing order failures.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If inventory contention is below 1% of orders

decision: Cart API endpoint conflict resolved: POST /api/cart for add, PUT /api/cart/{id} for upsert
chosen_approach: POST /api/cart creates or adds to cart; PUT /api/cart/{item_id} updates quantity for an existing item; DELETE /api/cart/{item_id} removes

rejected_alternatives.approach: Single PATCH /api/cart endpoint for all cart mutations
rejected_alternatives.reason: PATCH semantics are ambiguous for add-vs-update; separate verbs make client code and error handling clearer.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If cart operation count exceeds 3 distinct endpoints and clients request a unified mutation API

decision: Skip image optimization for v1 — Vercel auto-optimizes via next/image adequately
chosen_approach: Use next/image component with Vercel's built-in image optimization; no custom image pipeline or CDN transform layer

rejected_alternatives.approach: Cloudinary transform pipeline for all product images
rejected_alternatives.reason: Adds a third-party dependency and per-transform cost; Vercel's built-in optimization handles WebP conversion and responsive sizing.
rejected_alternatives.revisit_criterion: If LCP exceeds 2.5s on production product detail pages with high-res images

revisit_criterion: If LCP exceeds 2.5s on production product detail pages with high-res images

decision: Rate limit increase to 1000 req/min for load testing phase
chosen_approach: Temporarily raise Supabase rate limit to 1000 req/min during Phase 4 load testing; revert to 200 req/min before production deploy
